source_pr.title: executor: fix DATA RACE caused by the `vecGroupChecker`
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Fix https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/13937.

In the method `evalGroupItemsAndResolveGroups`, the `vecGroupChecker` allocates some temporal buffers to store intermediate data, and it keeps some references to these intermediate data after putting these buffers back, which results in DATA RACE.

### What is changed and how it works?
Make a copy of the data instead of keeping references to it.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

symptoms: executor: fix DATA RACE caused by the `vecGroupChecker`
failure_signature: executor: fix DATA RACE caused by the `vecGroupChecker`
root_cause_explanation: `vecGroupChecker` reused temporary column buffers and kept references to values after the buffers were returned/reused, which can lead to concurrent access and Go race detector warnings. The fix avoids retaining references by copying the relevant values (e.g., decimal/JSON/string) before storing them for later comparison.
fix_pattern: Do not keep references to data in reused scratch buffers; copy the value (or underlying bytes) before storing/using it later to avoid data races.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix DATA RACE caused by the `vecGroupChecker`
